mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals weaker consumer spending, causing equity valuations to contract over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, generating sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in industrial production signal accelerating corporate output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying in equity markets.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of observations during recovery phases, or the Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate profit margins and force central banks to tighten policy, systematically reducing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation months, or the correlation between CPI growth and SPX returns turns positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent, aggregate demand contraction forces corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive 60-day returns in more than 58 percent of triggered quarters, or the predictive accuracy fails to exceed random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates an optimal growth environment without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts signal tightening mortgage credit and weakening consumer balance sheets, triggering immediate risk-off selling in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power after 2008.
